entries.title: next_chapter() selection race window under concurrent creator run continue (single-user assumption)
entries.scope: crates/nexus-local-db/src/work_chapters.rs:511-530 (next_chapter); callers in daemon get_work enrichment + CLI continue path
entries.note: next_chapter() runs three SQL queries (now consolidated to one MIN query after F-001) without transaction/claim atomicity. Two concurrent creator run continue calls could both observe the same chapter before any transition has flipped its row to draft/finalized. Per spec §4.5.2 the query is the SSOT; novel.chapter_transition guards against from_status mismatch but the user may see duplicate in-progress schedules. Single-user local-first path is safe; race only under concurrent invocation. Document single-writer assumption or add try_claim_next_chapter (UPDATE ... RETURNING chapter) in a follow-up if multi-terminal use is common.
entries.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept-with-doc: local-first single-writer invariant. Doc on next_chapter() captures invariant + upgrade pattern. Commit 932097ea.

entries.title: Plan T9 surface of on-disk missing-file hints only partially delivered in CLI status
entries.note: Status surface shows DB rows + progress + next-action hint + comment acknowledging on-disk absence, but no explicit on-disk missing-file warning line (e.g. body_path absent for chNN). DB SSOT preserved; reconcile-chapters covers remediation. Add explicit check-and-emit in status arm in a follow-up.
entries.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept: CLI surface of on-disk missing-file hints out of P5 scope. Register R-V139P5-N1 for V1.40 UX follow-up.

entries.title: Write-on-read anti-pattern in GET /v1/local/works/{id} (lazy completion promotion)
entries.note: GET /v1/local/works/{id} mutates works.status to completed when is_work_completed() returns true. Violates REST semantics; two identical GET requests can return different status values. The handler comment acknowledges this as lazy promotion matching V1.36 P4 fix wave auto-promote pattern. Move promotion to finalize transition path or document lazy promotion explicitly in API contract.
entries.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept-with-doc + idempotency test. Lazy completion-promotion is intentional, now contractual. Commit 63b6ad59.

entries.title: WorkApiDto.chapters vector size uncapped for unusual total_planned_chapters
entries.note: list_chapters returns all rows with no limit. At typical novel scales (<=100 chapters) this is fine; not a practical concern since total_planned_chapters is user-controlled at init and local DB is not exposed to untrusted network clients. Add LIMIT or pagination in a follow-up if multi-volume works with100+ chapters become common.
entries.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept: chapters uncapped is theoretical DoS for local-first. Register R-V139P5-N2 for V1.40 UX.

entries.title: is_work_completed total_planned_chapters=NULL explicit test missing
entries.note: Code path returns false early for NULL or0 total_planned_chapters; doc comment correct. Add explicit test case to make the §6.1 guard behavior self-documenting.
entries.closure_note: V1.39 P5 added 2 hermetic unit tests covering NULL and 0 total_planned_chapters in crates/nexus-local-db/src/work_chapters.rs.
